context: AF & DR Class — DR grand jetés, développé devant, port de bras; AF frappés, port de bras, grand battement at barre

corrections.text: Look up and over instead of at the ground — for confidence
Need better battement in grand jetés
corrections.theme: Hip hiking in développés and battement

corrections.text: Square shoulders and hips in développé devant
corrections.theme: Hip hiking in développés and battement

corrections.text: Use upper body more and eyeline in port de bras
Memorise the frappé pattern without getting distracted
More accent and hold on the en croix in frappés
Losing accent and dynamic on the en dedans in frappés
Weight forward on the pivot in port de bras
Arms need to get to first and open to fourth faster to get around in port de bras
Think about bottom leg turning out in port de bras
Glissade: point second foot especially devant
Glissade should barely elevate but have a sense of lift
Step-unfold: unstable — cause not yet identified
Eyeline: wider fourth position in port de bras
Nail back heel down in the back bend
Eyeline follows hands in port de bras
Weight sitting back in grand battement devant
Don't curl toes in grand battement
Passé sickled and not activating glute to hold knee in place
Think about knee lifting instead of foot leading in passé
Toes need to scoop and articulate before going into passé
In coupé under and over for frappés and glissades: point both feet
Hands only lightly on the barre — no weight on it
Supporting side turned on, weight over toes, arches pulling up
